title: `_set_str` missing `NULL` check after `PyUnicode_DecodeASCII` in `_csv.c`
author: devdanzin
body:
# Crash report

### What happened?

It's possible to segfault the interpreter on OOM by trying to write a CSV row.

### Automated diagnosis:

Bug: On OOM, `PyUnicode_DecodeASCII` returns `NULL`, `_set_str` stores `NULL` in `*target` and returns 0 (success). Later `PyUnicode_GET_LENGTH(NULL)` segfaults.

File: Modules/_csv.c, line 319

MRE:
```python
import _testcapi, csv, io

# Persistent failure ensures the allocation inside _set_str fails
for n in range(1, 30):
    print(n)
    _testcapi.set_nomemory(n, 0)
    try:
        w = csv.writer(io.StringIO())
        w.writerow(["a", "b"])
        _testcapi.remove_mem_hooks()
        break
    except MemoryError:
        _testcapi.remove_mem_hooks()
```

Backtrace:

Found using [cpython-review-toolkit](https://github.com/devdanzin/cpython-review-toolkit) with Claude Opus 4.6, using the `/cpython-review-toolkit:explore Modules/_csv.c all deep` command.



### CPython versions tested on:

CPython main branch

### Operating systems tested on:

Linux

### Output from running 'python -VV' on the command line:

Python 3.15.0a7+ (heads/main:e0f7c1097e1, Mar 17 2026, 18:10:52) [Clang 21.1.2 (2ubuntu6)]

comments.author: vstinner
comments.body: I wrote https://github.com/python/cpython/pull/146113 to fix the issue.

comments.author: vstinner
comments.body: Issue fixed by https://github.com/python/cpython/pull/146113. Thanks for the bug report.

comments.author: smontanaro
comments.body: I'm certainly no expert on crashers, but would it have been possible to add a unit test for this? Even @devdanzin's report code would seem to have been helpful.

comments.author: vstinner
comments.body:
Causing PyUnicode_DecodeASCII() to fail with a memory allocation failure requires to tune `_testcapi.set_nomemory()` exactly, but the `set_nomemory()` argument can vary over time if Python internals change.

I don't think that we can write a reliable test with reasonable maintenance.
